title: Heaven Below

is an American [hard rock](/wiki/Hard_rock "Hard rock") band based in [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles"). The band's debut album *Countdown to Devil* was released March 2009.

**Heaven Below** is an American [hard rock](/wiki/Hard_rock "Hard rock") band based in [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles"). The band's debut album *Countdown to Devil* was released March 2009.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Heaven_Below&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The band was formed in late 2007 by [Patrick Kennison](/wiki/Patrick_Kennison "Patrick Kennison") ([Union Underground](/wiki/Union_Underground "Union Underground")), [Marty O'Brien](/wiki/Marty_O%27Brien "Marty O'Brien") ([Disturbed](/wiki/Disturbed_(band) "Disturbed (band)"), [We Are the Fallen](/wiki/We_Are_the_Fallen "We Are the Fallen")) and session drummer [Chad Clark](/wiki/Chad_Clark_(drummer) "Chad Clark (drummer)") to film a video for Kennison's song "The Laughing Dead". In 2008 guitarist Dave Comer was added completing the quartet. Later that year Comer and O'Brien were replaced by guitarist Jesse Billson and bassist John Younger respectively.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

### *Countdown to Devil*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Heaven_Below&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Countdown to Devil")]

Following the release of *[Countdown to Devil](/wiki/Countdown_to_Devil "Countdown to Devil")*, the group completed its first tour in mid-2009 dubbed the "Texas Takeover Tour".[[3]](#cite_note-3) Media coverage of the tour included a live performance on [FOX-TV](/wiki/KABB "KABB") in San Antonio, TX.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

In August 2010, the band released a [self-titled EP](/wiki/Heaven_Below_(EP) "Heaven Below (EP)"),[[5]](#cite_note-5) and led a tour dubbed the "Horns and Halos Tour" which spanned over much of the country and even Canada.[[6]](#cite_note-6) Before "[The Mirror Never Lies](/wiki/The_Mirror_Never_Lies_(song) "The Mirror Never Lies (song)")" was released, Chad Clark left the band and was replaced by [Julien-K](/wiki/Julien-K "Julien-K") and [Dead by Sunrise](/wiki/Dead_by_Sunrise "Dead by Sunrise") drummer Elias Andra.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

### *Falling from Zero*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Heaven_Below&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Falling from Zero")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1b/Patrick_Kennison.jpg/250px-Patrick_Kennison.jpg)](/wiki/File:Patrick_Kennison.jpg)

Patrick Kennison

In 2012, the band released their second studio album *[Falling from Zero](/wiki/Falling_from_Zero "Falling from Zero")*[[9]](#cite_note-blabbermouth1-9) followed in 2013 by compilation album *Dos Diablos Digital Box Set*, acoustic album *[The Deadlight Sessions](/wiki/The_Deadlight_Sessions "The Deadlight Sessions")*[[10]](#cite_note-spirit-of-metal1-10) and cover album *Sleeping Giants*.[[11]](#cite_note-11) It was during the same year that percussionist Shad Wilhelm joined the ensemble, replacing Andra.[[10]](#cite_note-spirit-of-metal1-10)

In the year following, the group advertised their limited edition coffin kits, containing an album entitled *Demonic Demos & Unsung Lullabies*. The CD, which contained previously unreleased tunes, an electric version of "Damaged" (published in 2013 on *[The Deadlight Sessions](/wiki/The_Deadlight_Sessions "The Deadlight Sessions")*) as well as a cover of "[Nothing Else Matters](/wiki/Nothing_Else_Matters "Nothing Else Matters")" by [Metallica](/wiki/Metallica "Metallica") was made available exclusively in the coffin kit.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

### *Good Morning Apocalypse*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Heaven_Below&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Good Morning Apocalypse")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f7/John_Younger_Heaven_Below.jpg/250px-John_Younger_Heaven_Below.jpg)](/wiki/File:John_Younger_Heaven_Below.jpg)

John Younger

In 2014, the band began working on what is to be their third [studio album](/wiki/Studio_album "Studio album"). In January 2015, the group posted a [photograph](/wiki/Photograph "Photograph") on their [internet site](/wiki/Internet_site "Internet site") with the words *[Good Morning Apocalypse](/wiki/Good_Morning_Apocalypse "Good Morning Apocalypse")*,[[13]](#cite_note-facebook1-13)[[14]](#cite_note-heavenbelow1-14)[[15]](#cite_note-facebook2-15) the title of their upcoming work.[[16]](#cite_note-schecterguitars.com-16)

Some time after mid-2014, Billson left the ensemble, with Lucas Kanopa replacing him some time later.[[16]](#cite_note-schecterguitars.com-16)

The band released the album on October 14, 2016.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

In late 2018, [Nikki Stringfield](/wiki/Nikki_Stringfield "Nikki Stringfield") of American rock bands [The Iron Maidens](/wiki/The_Iron_Maidens "The Iron Maidens") and [Femme Fatale](/wiki/Femme_Fatale_(band) "Femme Fatale (band)") became a touring guitarist for the ensemble.[[18]](#cite_note-18)[[19]](#cite_note-19)

In April 2020, Kennison announced the release date of May 17, 2020, for upcoming cover album *Rest in Pieces*.[[20]](#cite_note-auto-20)

On Sundays in February 2022, Stringfield and Kennison performed during their residency at [Hard Rock Cafe](/wiki/Hard_Rock_Cafe "Hard Rock Cafe") in [Hollywood](/wiki/Hollywood,_Los_Angeles "Hollywood, Los Angeles"), [California](/wiki/California "California").[[21]](#cite_note-21)[[22]](#cite_note-22)

### *The Majestic Twelve*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Heaven_Below&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: The Majestic Twelve")]

In late 2024, the ensemble plans to release *The Majestic Twelve*, their fourth studio album.[[23]](#cite_note-auto1-23) The album was released on July 22, 2025.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

## Members

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Heaven_Below&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Members")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c1/Shad_Wilhelm.jpg/250px-Shad_Wilhelm.jpg)](/wiki/File:Shad_Wilhelm.jpg)

Shad Wilhelm

* [Patrick Kennison](/wiki/Patrick_Kennison "Patrick Kennison") (vocals/guitars)
* [Nikki Stringfield](/wiki/Nikki_Stringfield "Nikki Stringfield") (guitars)[[16]](#cite_note-schecterguitars.com-16)
* John Younger (bass)[[9]](#cite_note-blabbermouth1-9)
* Shad Wilhelm (drums)[[10]](#cite_note-spirit-of-metal1-10)

## Discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Heaven_Below&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Discography")]

### Studio albums

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Heaven_Below&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Studio albums")]

* *[Countdown to Devil](/wiki/Countdown_to_Devil "Countdown to Devil")* (2009)
* *[Falling From Zero](/wiki/Falling_From_Zero "Falling From Zero")* (2012)
* *[Good Morning Apocalypse](/wiki/Good_Morning_Apocalypse "Good Morning Apocalypse")* (2016)
* *The Majestic Twelve* (2025)
* *Alternate Abductions* (2026)[[25]](#cite_note-25)

### Other albums

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Heaven_Below&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Other albums")]

* *We Sold Our Soul for Heaven Below* (2008)
* *Infamy (EP)* (2008)[[26]](#cite_note-26)
* *Reworking the Devil* (2009)[[27]](#cite_note-27)[[28]](#cite_note-28)
* *[Heaven Below](/wiki/Heaven_Below_(EP) "Heaven Below (EP)")* (2010)[[29]](#cite_note-29)
* *Unleashed in the West* (2011)[[30]](#cite_note-30)
* *[The Mirror Never Lies: Mega-Single](/wiki/The_Mirror_Never_Lies:_Mega-Single "The Mirror Never Lies: Mega-Single")* (2011)[[31]](#cite_note-31)
* *Dos Diablos Digital Box Set* (2013)
* *[The Deadlight Sessions](/wiki/The_Deadlight_Sessions "The Deadlight Sessions")* (2013)
* *Sleeping Giants* (2013)[[32]](#cite_note-32)
* *Demonic Demos & Unsung Lullabies* (2014)[[33]](#cite_note-33)
* *Rest in Pieces* (2020)[[20]](#cite_note-auto-20)

### Songs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Heaven_Below&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Songs")]

* "[Major Tom (Coming Home)](/wiki/Major_Tom_(Coming_Home) "Major Tom (Coming Home)")" - originally released in 1982 by [Peter Schilling](/wiki/Peter_Schilling "Peter Schilling").
* "[Twilight Zone](/wiki/Twilight_Zone_(Golden_Earring_song) "Twilight Zone (Golden Earring song)")" - originally released in 1982 by [Golden Earring](/wiki/Golden_Earring "Golden Earring"). This track was never officially released. After the release of their self-titled EP, all who bought a copy were able to download *Twilight Zone* from the internet through a special disc feature.
* "[Heartbreaker](/wiki/Heartbreaker_(Pat_Benatar_song) "Heartbreaker (Pat Benatar song)")" - originally released by [Pat Benatar](/wiki/Pat_Benatar "Pat Benatar") in 1979.
* "[Subdivisions](/wiki/Subdivisions_(song) "Subdivisions (song)")" - originally released by [Rush](/wiki/Rush_(band) "Rush (band)") in 1982. Featuring [William Shatner](/wiki/William_Shatner "William Shatner") speaking "Subdivisions" as well as "Attention all planets of the solar federation - we have assumed control" from the song [2112](/wiki/2112_(song) "2112 (song)").
